check_var_format.description: Selects the output format for check_var reports. Supported formats are esv, csv, wsv, and vo.

circle_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_CIRCLE objects are rendered in pMarineViewer. Setting this false hides circle geometry without affecting labels for other object classes.

circle_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to VIEW_CIRCLE objects are rendered. The circle geometry remains controlled separately by circle_viewable_all.

collision_dist.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle minimum distance, in meters, below which an encounter is classified as a collision.

collision_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when an inter-vehicle CPA event is classified as a collision. Flag values may use event macros such as $CPA, $V1, and $V2.
Adds a variable-value flag posted when a vehicle-obstacle encounter is classified as a collision. Flag values may use distance and vehicle/obstacle macros from the app.

collision_range.description: Sets the inter-vehicle closest-point-of-approach distance, in meters, at or below which an encounter is classified as a collision.
Deprecated documentation alias for collision_dist; use collision_dist in mission files for the local source version.

colormap.description: Associates a MOOS variable, source, or community substring with a terminal color in uXMS output.

comms_pulse_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_COMMS_PULSE communication pulses are rendered. Setting this false suppresses comms-pulse visuals while leaving the underlying MOOS mail untouched.

comms_range.description: Sets the maximum range, in meters, for sharing inter-vehicle node reports and node messages. The value nolimit disables range cutoff.

condition.description: Adds a logic condition that must be true before echo and flip publications are made. When false, messages may be held depending on hold_messages.
Adds a logic condition that must evaluate true before collision monitoring and event reporting are active.
Adds a pass condition; this is an alias for pass_condition. All pass conditions must be true for uQueryDB to return success.
Adds a logic condition that must be true before the script is unpaused and allowed to post ready events.

contact_local_coords.description: Selects the policy for filling local x/y coordinates in incoming node reports from latitude/longitude. Legal modes include verbatim, lazy_lat_lon, and force_lat_lon; verbatim leaves incoming reports unchanged.

contact_max_age.description: Sets how many seconds may pass without a fresh contact report before the contact is retired and dropped from the active contact list. The current pContactMgrV20 source initializes this to 60 seconds.

content_mode.description: Selects the startup content shown in the infocast panes. Supported modes include appcast and realmcast.
Sets the startup infocast content mode shown by uMACView, either appcast or realmcast.
Selects which uXMS report view is shown at startup. Supported modes include scoping, history, procs, and help.

coord_policy_global.description: When true, node reports publish only latitude/longitude coordinates and omit local x/y coordinates. The crossfill policy controls how lat/lon values are obtained.

cpa_range_color.description: Sets the default display color for CPA range circles. This color is used when an alert rule does not specify its own CPA range color.

critical_range.description: Sets the range within which node reports are shared for safety even when normal group or range rules would block them.

crossfill_policy.description: Controls how pNodeReporter fills missing local x/y coordinates from lat/lon or missing lat/lon coordinates from local x/y. Supported policies include literal, fill-empty, use-latest, and global.

current_field.description: Loads a file describing a spatial current field for the simulator. When active, the current field contributes environmental drift to simulated vehicle motion.

current_field_active.description: Enables or disables use of the configured current field. This lets a mission keep the current-field file configured while toggling whether the simulator applies it.

datum_color.description: Sets the color of the local geodesy datum marker drawn by pMarineViewer. Use any supported MOOS-IvP color name.

datum_size.description: Sets the rendered point size of the local geodesy datum marker. Larger values make the datum more visible on the viewer background.

datum_viewable.description: Controls whether pMarineViewer renders the local geodesy datum marker. This only affects viewer display; the LatOrigin/LongOrigin mission datum remains unchanged.

deadflag.description: Defines one MOOS variable-value pair posted when the heartbeat has been missing longer than max_noheart.

debug.description: Enables extra terminal debugging output from uFldNodeComms.

decay.description: Controls extrapolation of stale contact reports as start,end seconds. Before the start time the last reported speed/heading are used, between start and end the speed decays toward zero, and 0,0 disables extrapolation.

default_beacon_color.description: Sets the default marker color used when rendering beacons.

default_beacon_freq.description: Sets the default unsolicited report interval for beacons. The value may be never, a fixed number of seconds, or a random range such as 60:120.

default_beacon_pull_dist.description: Sets the default distance a vehicle-side ping can reach when querying beacons.

default_beacon_push_dist.description: Sets the default distance from a beacon within which its unsolicited or solicited range report can be received.

default_beacon_shape.description: Sets the default marker shape used when rendering beacons.

default_beacon_width.description: Sets the default rendered marker width for beacons.

default_color.description: Sets the default vehicle color used in spoofed node reports when a request omits color.

default_duration.description: Sets the default duration, in seconds, for repeated spoofed node reports when a request omits duration. A negative value means no automatic duration limit.

default_group.description: Sets the default group field used in spoofed node reports when a request omits group. Empty string means no group is added.

default_heading.description: Sets the default heading, in degrees, used in spoofed node reports when a request omits heading.

default_hostip.description: Sets the fallback IP address pHostInfo posts when it cannot determine an address from the machine interfaces.

default_hostip_force.description: Forces pHostInfo to use the configured fallback IP address instead of an auto-discovered network-interface address. This is mainly useful for debugging or controlled simulations.

default_length.description: Sets the default vehicle length, in meters, used in spoofed node reports when a request omits length.

default_rate.description: Sets the default speech rate used when an incoming SAY_MOOS utterance does not specify its own rate. The value is passed to the speech backend as the rate for spoken text.

default_spd.description: Sets the default speed used in spoofed node reports when a request omits speed.

default_voice.description: Sets the voice used for spoken utterances when a SAY_MOOS request omits the voice field. Available voice names depend on the platform speech backend.

default_vsource.description: Sets the default vehicle source included in spoofed node reports when a spoof request does not provide vsource.

default_vtype.description: Sets the default vehicle type used in spoofed node reports when a request omits vtype.

default_water_depth.description: Sets the default local water depth, in meters, used for altitude calculations. A value of zero means no known water depth, so altitude is not reported from depth alone.

delay_reset.description: Adds a delay, or random delay range, to each event time on every script reset after the first pass.

delay_start.description: Adds a delay, or random delay range, to each event time on the first script pass only.

disable_flag.description: Posts a MOOS variable-value flag whenever a contact is disabled. Use this for mission logic or logging that needs to observe disable events.

disable_var.description: Names the MOOS variable pContactMgrV20 listens to for contact-disable requests. If it is unset, external disable requests are not accepted.

display_all.description: When true, uXMS shows all MOOSDB variables in scoping mode rather than only the configured watch variables.

display_aux_source.description: When true, uXMS shows non-empty auxiliary source information in the source column instead of the ordinary source app name.

display_bhv_posts.description: Controls whether the behavior-posts section is shown in the uHelmScope report.

display_community.description: Controls whether the Community column is shown in scoping and history reports.

display_history_var.description: Controls whether the variable name is shown on each row in history mode. Turning it off saves horizontal space when the history view is scoped to one variable.

display_moos_scope.description: Controls whether the MOOS variable scope section is shown in the uHelmScope report.

display_radii.description: Enables posting visual range circles for configured alert and CPA radii. This affects viewer output and does not change alert triggering.

display_radii_id.description: Selects which configured alert id should have its alert/CPA radii rendered when display_radii is enabled. This matters when multiple alert definitions are present.

display_source.description: Controls whether the Source column is shown in scoping and history reports.

display_time.description: Controls whether the Time column is shown in scoping and history reports.

display_virgins.description: Controls whether scoped MOOS variables that have not yet received mail are displayed.
Controls whether variables that have never been written, or have empty-string values, are shown in uXMS reports.

draw_region.description: Controls whether the configured obstacle-generation region is posted as a VIEW_POLYGON for visualization.

drift_vector.description: Sets the environmental drift as heading,magnitude. The simulator converts this vector into x/y drift components and applies it to vehicle motion.

drift_x.description: Sets the environmental drift component in the local x direction, in meters per second. This drift is added to simulated vehicle position updates.

drift_y.description: Sets the environmental drift component in the local y direction, in meters per second. This drift is added to simulated vehicle position updates.

drop_point_coords.description: Controls how drop-point labels show coordinates. Supported values include as-dropped, lat-lon, and local-grid.

drop_point_vertex_size.description: Sets the point size used to render drop points. Legal values are integer sizes in the supported viewer range.

drop_point_viewable_all.description: Controls whether user drop-point markers are rendered in the viewer. This affects the visual display of drop points, not whether drop-point mail can be generated.

earange.description: Extends the range at which this vehicle can hear node reports from other vehicles. Values are in the range 1 to 10, with 1 as the default range multiplier.

early_warning_flag.description: Posts a MOOS variable-value flag when an early warning is triggered before the full contact alert condition. Use this to react before the alert range or CPA condition is reached.

early_warning_radii.description: Enables posting a visual range circle for the early-warning distance. This is a viewer aid for warning geometry and does not itself trigger alerts.

early_warning_time.description: Sets how many seconds before the predicted contact alert an early-warning event should be posted. A negative value leaves early warnings disabled.

echo.description: Maps one source MOOS variable to another destination variable so incoming mail on the source is re-posted under the destination name.

echo_latest_only.description: When true, pEchoVar posts only the most recent received value for an echoed variable on each iteration instead of replaying every queued message.

enable_flag.description: Posts a MOOS variable-value flag whenever a contact is enabled. Use this for mission logic or logging that needs to observe re-enable events.

enable_var.description: Names the MOOS variable pContactMgrV20 listens to for contact-enable requests. If it is unset, external enable requests are not accepted.

encounter_dist.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle distance, in meters, at which obstacle encounters begin being tracked.

encounter_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when an inter-vehicle CPA event enters the encounter range.
Adds a variable-value flag posted when a vehicle comes within the configured encounter distance of an obstacle.

encounter_range.description: Sets the inter-vehicle CPA distance, in meters, at or below which a pair is tracked as an encounter. The local source default is 20 meters.
Deprecated documentation alias for encounter_dist; use encounter_dist in mission files for the local source version.

event.description: Defines one timer-script event, including at minimum the variable, value, and event time or time range to post.

ewarn_radii_color.description: Sets the display color for the early-warning range circle. This affects viewer output when early_warning_radii is enabled.

exponential_decay.description: Enables exponentially decaying detection probability beyond the combined reach and reply distances.

extrap_enabled.description: Enables sparse node-report posting based on extrapolation. When enabled, reports may be held back until movement, heading change, or max-gap thresholds require a fresh report.

extrap_hdg_thresh.description: Sets the heading-change threshold, in degrees, that forces a new node report when extrapolated reporting is enabled.

extrap_max_gap.description: Sets the maximum time, in seconds, that may pass without posting a node report when extrapolated reporting is enabled.

extrap_pos_thresh.description: Sets the position-change threshold, in meters, that forces a new node report when extrapolated reporting is enabled.

fail_condition.description: Adds a logic condition that causes the mission evaluation to fail if it is satisfied.
Adds a logic condition that causes uQueryDB to return failure if it becomes true. Any satisfied fail condition is enough to fail the query.

fail_flag.description: Defines one MOOS variable-value post made after pMissionEval determines that the mission did not pass.

finish_val.description: Sets the value that must be observed on finish_var before uMayFinish allows completion.

finish_var.description: Names the MOOS variable uMayFinish monitors to decide whether the mission may finish.

flag.description: Defines one MOOS variable-value post that pAutoPoke will publish once the required node count has been reached.

flip.description: Defines a pEchoVar flip rule that extracts or transforms components from a source variable and posts the result to a destination variable.

forward_var.description: Names the MOOS variable used to cue uTimerScript to skip ahead in script time.

full_screen.description: Starts pMarineViewer in full-screen geo-display mode, hiding appcast/realmcast panes and bottom fields. This is a display layout setting.

grid_config.description: Supplies the search-grid geometry, cell layout, label, and initial values used by pSearchGrid when it creates the grid.

grid_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_GRID and VIEW_GRID_DELTA objects are rendered. Setting this false hides grid overlays while preserving other geometry classes.

grid_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels on rendered grid objects are shown. Grid geometry visibility remains controlled by grid_viewable_all.

grid_viewable_opaqueness.description: Sets the opacity of rendered grid objects. Values range from 0 for transparent to 1 for fully opaque.

ground_true.description: Documentation spelling for ground_truth. When true, publishes ground-truth range reports with noise removed for diagnostics.

ground_truth.description: When true, publishes ground-truth beacon range reports in addition to noisy simulated reports when random noise is enabled.

groups.description: When true, shares inter-vehicle node reports only between vehicles in the same group, except within the critical range.

hash_delta.description: Sets the spacing, in meters, between background hash/grid lines. Common supported spacings include 50, 100, 200, 500, and 1000.

hash_shade.description: Sets the grayscale shade used for hash marks. Values range from 0 for black to 1 for white.

hash_viewable.description: Controls whether background hash/grid lines are rendered over the operating area.

hdg_error.description: Adds a test offset, in degrees, to the heading field in generated node reports. This is intended for testing downstream consumers with inconsistent heading/course data.

heartbeat_var.description: Names the MOOS variable treated as the heartbeat. Each received posting resets the missing-heartbeat timer.

history.description: Sets how many recent node records uFldPathCheck keeps for each vehicle when estimating present speed and checking path progress.

history_var.description: Names the single MOOS variable shown in uXMS history mode. If multiple history_var lines are provided, the last one is used.

hold_messages.description: When true, pEchoVar retains incoming messages received while conditions are false and processes them once conditions become true.

ignore_group.description: Adds one or more contact groups to the global ignore filter. Contacts in ignored groups are excluded from contact-manager alert/range processing.
Ignores encounters only when both vehicles are in one of the listed groups.
Ignores incoming node reports from contacts whose group matches this configured group.
Ignores incoming node reports from contacts whose group matches this configured group.

ignore_name.description: Adds one or more contact names to the global ignore filter. Contacts with ignored names are excluded from contact-manager alert/range processing.

ignore_range.description: Ignores incoming obstacle points farther than this range from ownship. A negative value disables range-based ignoring.

ignore_region.description: Adds a polygon region where contacts are ignored by the global filter. Use this to suppress alerts for contacts inside a known safe or irrelevant operating area.

ignore_type.description: Adds one or more contact types to the global ignore filter. Contacts reporting ignored types are excluded from contact-manager alert/range processing.

infocast_font_size.description: Sets the font size for the named pMarineViewer pane. Supported values are xsmall, small, medium, large, and xlarge.
Sets the font size used in the infocast content panes.

infocast_height.description: Sets the height of the lower appcast/realmcast pane as a percentage of the pMarineViewer window. Supported values are stepped from 30 through 90.
Sets the height of the infocast display pane. Supported values are 30 through 90 in increments of 5.

infocast_viewable.description: Controls whether the infocast pane set is visible on the left side of the viewer. This affects layout only.

infocast_width.description: Sets the width of the appcast/realmcast pane area as a percentage of the pMarineViewer window width. Supported values are stepped from 20 through 70.

interval_policy.description: Selects whether the minimum interval between utterances is measured from the start or the end of the previous utterance. Supported values are from_start and from_end.

json_report.description: Controls JSON node-report publication. When true, the node report is published in JSON format; when set to another variable name, JSON is posted there while normal CSP output continues.

keyword.description: Requires incoming NODE_BROKER_PING messages to contain this keyword before uFldShoreBroker responds.

lasso.description: If true, each obstacle cluster is represented as a regular polygon rather than the convex hull of retained points.

lasso_points.description: Sets the number of vertices in the regular lasso polygon used when lasso mode is enabled.

lasso_radius.description: Sets the radius, in meters, of the regular lasso polygon used when lasso mode is enabled.

layout.description: Defines an alternate report table layout by selecting which fields are shown in each uFldScope row.

lclick_ix_start.description: Sets the starting index value for left-click context-menu macros that expand indexed click actions. This is useful when generating numbered postings from repeated mouse clicks.

lead_condition.description: Adds a logic condition that must become true before the associated pass/fail aspect is evaluated.

left_context.description: Defines a left-click context menu command that posts a MOOS variable-value pair using mouse-position macros such as $(XPOS), $(YPOS), $(LAT), and $(LON).

load_summary.description: Names the MOOS variable where uFldObstacleSim posts a summary of loaded obstacle information.

log_the_image.description: Requests that pLogger copy the active background image and related info file into the mission log set. This helps alogview later reproduce the viewer background.

marker.description: Defines a static marker using the same comma-separated field syntax as VIEW_MARKER messages. A marker needs a type and a position, with optional label, color, width, and message fields.

marker_edge_width.description: Sets the width of the black outer edge drawn around VIEW_MARKER objects.

marker_scale.description: Applies a global scale factor to marker rendering. This changes visual marker size without changing marker positions.

marker_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_MARKER objects are rendered. Marker label display is controlled separately by marker_viewable_labels.

marker_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to VIEW_MARKER objects are rendered. Marker geometry remains controlled separately by marker_viewable_all.

match_group.description: Adds one or more contact groups to the global match filter. When match filters are configured, contacts must match the accepted group criteria to be processed.
Restricts obstacle simulation or Voronoi processing to vessels matching this group name.

match_name.description: Adds one or more contact names to the global match filter. When configured, only contacts with accepted names pass this part of filtering.

match_region.description: Adds a polygon region that contacts must match to pass the global filter. Contacts outside the matched region are ignored for contact-manager processing.

match_type.description: Adds one or more contact types to the global match filter. When configured, only contacts with accepted types pass this part of filtering.

max_acceleration.description: Limits the simulated acceleration rate in meters per second squared. A zero value disables acceleration limiting.

max_age_per_point.description: Sets how long, in seconds, a tracked feature point may remain in memory before it is dropped.

max_db_uptime.description: Sets the MOOSDB uptime, in seconds, after which uMayFinish exits regardless of finish_var state. A negative value disables this timeout.

max_deceleration.description: Limits the simulated deceleration rate in meters per second squared. This caps how quickly commanded thrust changes can slow the vehicle.

max_depth_rate.description: Limits how quickly simulated depth can change, in meters per second. This cap applies to the vertical-motion model.

max_depth_rate_speed.description: Sets the vehicle speed at which the configured maximum depth-change rate is achievable. It shapes how speed affects vertical maneuvering authority.

max_msg_length.description: Sets the maximum number of characters allowed in the string component of an inter-vehicle node message.

max_noheart.description: Sets how many seconds a heartbeat may be absent before pDeadManPost posts the deadflags.

max_pts_per_cluster.description: Sets the maximum number of tracked feature points retained for each obstacle cluster.

max_retired_hist.description: Limits how many retired contacts are listed in CONTACTS_RETIRED history. Legal values are clamped to the supported range, and retired contacts are still reported at least once.

mhash_short_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where pMissionHash posts the short mission hash. The local source initializes this to MHASH; setting it to off disables the short-hash post.

min_duration.description: Sets the lower bound, in seconds, for randomized obstacle lifetime. A negative value disables randomized expiration.

min_msg_interval.description: Sets the minimum time, in seconds, between node-message sends from any one source vehicle.

min_rpt_interval.description: Sets the minimum time, in seconds, between node-report sends from any one source vehicle. A negative value disables report dropping.

min_utter_interval.description: Sets the minimum number of seconds between queued utterances. iSay will not start the next utterance until this interval permits it.

mission_form.description: Sets the mission-form name used when expanding MISSION_FORM macros in generated reports.

mission_hash_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where pMissionHash posts the full mission hash. Set this to off to disable full-hash posting.

msg_color.description: Sets the color used for inter-vehicle message communication pulses.

msg_flag.description: Defines a MOOS variable-value post made when a valid incoming message is accepted and posted to the local MOOSDB.

msg_groups.description: When true, shares inter-vehicle node messages only between vehicles in the same group.

msg_max_hist.description: Sets how many recent messages pRealm keeps per variable for string-message history display.

msg_repeat_color.description: Sets the color used for communication pulses representing repeated previously dropped node messages.

nav_grace_period.description: Sets the startup grace period, in seconds, before pNodeReporter warns about missing navigation inputs such as NAV_LAT or NAV_LONG. A negative value disables the grace limit.

near_breach_thresh.description: Sets the fractional point between normal load and the configured breach threshold where uLoadWatch begins reporting near-breaches. For example, 0.9 reports when the gap reaches 90% of the way to the breach threshold.

near_miss_dist.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle minimum distance, in meters, below which an encounter is classified as a near miss unless it is already a collision.

near_miss_flag.description: Adds a variable-value flag posted when an inter-vehicle CPA event is classified as a near miss.
Adds a variable-value flag posted when a vehicle-obstacle encounter is classified as a near miss.

near_miss_range.description: Sets the inter-vehicle CPA distance, in meters, at or below which an encounter is classified as a near miss unless it is already a collision.
Deprecated documentation alias for near_miss_dist; use near_miss_dist in mission files for the local source version.

node_pull_dist.description: Sets a vehicle-specific pull distance for vehicle-to-beacon range requests, overriding the default node pull distance for that vehicle.

node_push_dist.description: Sets a vehicle-specific push distance for beacon range reports, overriding the default node push distance for that vehicle.

node_report_output.description: Names the MOOS variable used for the local node report output. The conventional default is NODE_REPORT_LOCAL.

nodes_font_size.description: Sets the font size for the named pMarineViewer pane. Supported values are xsmall, small, medium, large, and xlarge.
Sets the font size used in the nodes pane.

nohelm_threshold.description: Sets how many seconds of quiet helm output are tolerated before pNodeReporter reports the helm as AWOL. This affects the helm-status fields in node reporting.

nowatch.description: Names process patterns that uProcessWatch should ignore even if they appear in DB_CLIENTS or the Antler-launched process list.

obstacle_file.description: Loads obstacle polygons from the named file. The file supplies obstacle geometry used by the simulator before posting KNOWN_OBSTACLE or GIVEN_OBSTACLE output.

obstacles_color.description: Sets the default display color for obstacle polygons posted by pObstacleMgr.

oparea.description: Defines the operation-area boundary for optional rendering. The value is a polygon specification in the same style used by other MOOS-IvP geometry objects.
oparea.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_pmviewer.pdf: datum and oparea rendering

oparea_viewable_all.description: Controls whether operating-area polygons are rendered. This is useful for temporarily hiding the op-area overlay without changing mission geometry.

oparea_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to operating-area geometry are rendered. The operating-area geometry remains controlled by oparea_viewable_all.

os_mode.description: Selects which operating-system speech backend iSay should use for speech and audio playback. Supported values are osx, linux, and both.

pass_condition.description: Adds a logic condition that must be true for the mission, or the current evaluation aspect, to pass.
Adds a logic condition that must be true for uQueryDB to return success. All configured pass conditions are evaluated together.

pass_flag.description: Defines one MOOS variable-value post made after pMissionEval determines that the mission passed.

pause_var.description: Names the MOOS variable used to receive pause, unpause, or toggle commands.

paused.description: Starts or places pNodeReporter in a paused state, suspending report publication while still allowing the app to run.
Controls whether uHelmScope starts in paused display mode.
Controls whether the script starts paused.

ping_color.description: Sets the visual color used for initiating range-request pulses.

ping_payments.description: Controls when a vehicle ping consumes its ping-wait interval. Supported modes include upon_request, upon_accept, and upon_response.

ping_wait.description: Sets the minimum number of seconds enforced between range-sensor pings.

plat_report_input.description: Adds one MOOS variable to the optional platform report, with optional fields such as gap and alias. The platform report is posted only when configured inputs require an update.

plat_report_output.description: Names the MOOS variable used for optional platform-report output. The conventional default is PLATFORM_REPORT_LOCAL.

platform_aspect.description: Adds a static param=value rider field to generated node reports. If the same field is configured more than once, the later value replaces the earlier one.

platform_beam.description: Sets the reported platform beam, in meters, included in node reports.

platform_color.description: Sets the color hint included in node reports for viewers such as pMarineViewer and alogview.

platform_group.description: Sets the group label included in generated node reports. This is useful for team or fleet grouping in downstream viewers and contact tools.

platform_length.description: Sets the reported platform length, in meters, included in node reports. Some known platform types may imply a rough default length when no length is configured.

platform_transparency.description: Sets the transparency hint included in node reports for viewers. Values range from 0 for nearly transparent to 1 for fully opaque.

platform_type.description: Sets the reported platform type included in node reports, such as kayak, uuv, auv, ship, or glider.

point_var.description: Names the MOOS variable from which pObstacleMgr reads tracked feature points.

point_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_POINT objects are rendered. Point labels remain controlled separately by point_viewable_labels.

point_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to VIEW_POINT objects are rendered. Point geometry remains controlled separately by point_viewable_all.

poly_duration.description: Sets how long, in seconds, a posted Voronoi proximity-cell polygon remains active for rendering.

poly_edge_color.description: Sets the edge color used when rendered obstacle polygons are posted for visualization.

poly_edge_size.description: Sets the rendered edge width for obstacle polygons.

poly_fill_color.description: Sets the fill color used when rendered obstacle polygons are posted for visualization.

poly_label_color.description: Sets the label color used when rendered obstacle polygons are posted for visualization.

poly_label_thresh.description: When the obstacle count exceeds this threshold, pObstacleMgr hides polygon labels to reduce viewer load.

poly_shade_thresh.description: When the obstacle count exceeds this threshold, pObstacleMgr hides polygon fill shading to reduce viewer load.

poly_transparency.description: Sets the rendered fill transparency for obstacle polygons; larger values make the fill more transparent.

poly_vert_color.description: Sets the vertex color used when rendered obstacle polygons are posted for visualization.

poly_vert_size.description: Sets the rendered vertex size for obstacle polygons.

poly_vertex_thresh.description: When the obstacle count exceeds this threshold, pObstacleMgr sets polygon vertex size to zero to reduce viewer load.

polygon_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_POLYGON objects are rendered. Polygon labels remain controlled separately by polygon_viewable_labels.

polygon_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to VIEW_POLYGON objects are rendered. Polygon geometry remains controlled separately by polygon_viewable_all.

post_all_ranges.description: Posts ordered range reports for all current contacts. This is a reporting option and can increase MOOSDB traffic when many contacts are present.

post_closest_range.description: Posts the range to the closest current contact, rounded to the nearest meter. This is a reporting option and does not affect alert logic.

post_dist_to_polys.description: Controls publication of ownship-to-obstacle range reports: true posts all, close posts only within alert range, and false disables them.

post_mapping.description: Maps one uProcessWatch posting variable to another output variable name.

post_points.description: Switches from posting full obstacle polygons to posting simulated sensor points from obstacle edges.

post_policy.description: Controls how deadflags are posted after the heartbeat fails. Supported policies are once, repeat, and reset.

post_view_polys.description: Controls whether pObstacleMgr posts VIEW_POLYGON messages for the obstacle polygons it is managing.

post_visuals.description: Controls whether uFldObstacleSim posts VIEW_POINT and VIEW_POLYGON visualization messages in addition to obstacle data.

prefer_inerface.description: Documentation typo for prefer_interface. The intended setting selects the preferred network interface when multiple valid IP addresses are available.
prefer_inerface.reviewSource: MOOS-IvP app_phostinfo.pdf: Listing 2.1 typo; moos-ivp/ivp/src/pHostInfo/HostInfo_Info.cpp:103 documents prefer_interface

prefix.description: Sets the prefix used for navigation variables published by the simulator, such as PREFIX_X, PREFIX_Y, PREFIX_HEADING, and PREFIX_SPEED. The default source prefix is USM.

procs_font_size.description: Sets the font size for the named pMarineViewer pane. Supported values are xsmall, small, medium, large, and xlarge.
Sets the font size used in the processes pane.

prox_edge_color.description: Sets the edge color for rendered Voronoi proximity-cell polygons.

prox_fill_color.description: Sets the fill color for rendered Voronoi proximity-cell polygons.

prox_label_color.description: Sets the label color for rendered Voronoi proximity-cell polygons.

prox_vertex_color.description: Sets the vertex color for rendered Voronoi proximity-cell polygons.

pulse_duration.description: Sets how long, in seconds, the VIEW_RANGE_PULSE remains visible for collision or near-miss events.
Sets how long communication pulses remain visible in viewers such as pMarineViewer.

pulse_range.description: Sets the radius, in meters, of the visual range pulse posted for collision or near-miss events.

pulse_render.description: Controls whether collision and near-miss events produce VIEW_RANGE_PULSE visual markers.

qbridge.description: Adds shorthand bridge rules for both variable_$V and variable_ALL forms to vehicle communities.

rand_var.description: Declares a random variable macro that can be expanded in event values.

range_pulse_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_RANGE_PULSE objects are rendered. Setting this false hides range-pulse effects such as collision or sensor pulses.

rate_points.description: Sets how many simulated sensor points are generated per obstacle on each iteration when post_points is enabled.

rclick_ix_start.description: Sets the starting index value for right-click context-menu macros that expand indexed click actions. This lets right-click generated postings use a separate index sequence.

reach_distance.description: Sets the distance out to which the pinging vehicle can be heard. The value may be numeric meters or nolimit.

realmcast_color_scheme.description: Sets the color scheme used for realmcast panes in pMarineViewer. Supported schemes include default, indigo, beige, white, and hillside.
Sets the color scheme used by realmcast panes.

realmcast_show_community.description: Controls whether the Community column is shown in realmcast output. Hiding it can make realmcast rows more compact.
Controls whether the Community column is shown in realmcast output.

realmcast_show_masked.description: Controls whether masked or filtered variables are included in realmcast output. This affects display filtering only, not realmcast collection.
Controls whether masked or filtered realmcast variables are shown.

realmcast_show_source.description: Controls whether the Source column is shown in realmcast output. Hiding it can conserve horizontal space in the realmcast pane.
Controls whether the Source column is shown in realmcast output.

realmcast_show_subscriptions.description: Controls whether the subscription list is included in realmcast output. Hiding it can reduce clutter when only publications are being inspected.
Controls whether the Subscriptions block is shown in realmcast output.

realmcast_time_format_utc.description: Controls whether realmcast time values are displayed as UTC time instead of elapsed local time.
Controls whether realmcast time values are displayed as UTC time instead of elapsed local time.

realmcast_trunc_content.description: Controls whether long realmcast value text is truncated in the value column.
Controls whether long realmcast value text is truncated in the value column.

realmcast_wrap_content.description: Controls whether long realmcast value text wraps onto multiple lines. Wrapping improves readability at the cost of vertical space.
Controls whether long realmcast value text wraps across multiple lines.

recap_interval.description: Sets the minimum time, in seconds, between CONTACTS_RECAP postings. Use off to suppress recap posting when it is too verbose for logging.

refresh_interval.description: Sets the minimum interval, in seconds, between node report publications for an active spoof request.
Sets how often GIVEN_OBSTACLE postings are refreshed. A negative value disables periodic refresh.

refresh_mode.description: Controls how pMarineViewer requests appcasts from apps. Supported modes include paused, events, and streaming.
Sets whether uMACView refreshes on events, streams continuously, or starts paused.
Controls when uXMS refreshes terminal output. Supported modes are events, streaming, and paused.

region.description: Supplies the convex operating polygon within which uFldObstacleSim places random or regenerated obstacles.

region_edge_color.description: Sets the edge color used when the obstacle-generation region is rendered.

reject_group.description: Completely disregards encounters involving any vehicle in one of the listed groups.

reject_range.description: Sets a range, in meters, beyond which new contacts are ignored and existing contacts may be dropped. This is a guard against tracking unbounded distant contacts.

relcast_interval.description: Sets the minimum time, in seconds, between generated REALMCAST reports. Values are clipped to the supported range.

reply_color.description: Deprecated alias for echo_color. Sets the visual color used for reply/echo range pulses.

reply_distance.description: Sets the distance out to which the pinged contact can reply. The value may be numeric meters or nolimit.

report_all_encounters.description: If true, UCD_REPORT is posted for clear encounters as well as near misses and collisions.

report_cog.description: Adds calculated course-over-ground information to node reports. The value is computed from recent position changes rather than the raw heading field.

report_column.description: Defines one output column in the mission-evaluation report file.

report_file.description: Names the file where pMissionEval writes the configured report columns and final mission result.

report_line_format.description: Selects the separator format used between mission-evaluation report columns.

report_vars.description: Selects which contact range report variable style is posted. Supported values are short, long, and both.

required_nodes.description: Sets how many nodes must be detected before pAutoPoke publishes its configured flags. A value of 0 allows the flags to be posted without waiting for node reports.

reset_interval.description: Sets the interval, in seconds, between automatic obstacle-field resets. A negative value disables automatic resets.

reset_max.description: Sets the maximum number of script resets allowed. Use a non-negative integer or nolimit.

reset_range.description: Requires all vehicles to be at least this many meters outside the obstacle region before a pending reset is allowed.

reset_time.description: Sets when the script resets: none disables time resets, all-posted resets after all events, or a numeric value resets at that script time.

reset_var.description: Names the MOOS variable used to receive explicit script reset commands.

result_flag.description: Defines one MOOS variable-value post made once pMissionEval has determined the mission result, regardless of pass or fail.

reuse_ids.description: Controls whether regenerated obstacles reuse previous obstacle labels across resets. If false, each reset generates new labels.

rider.description: Adds a dynamic rider field to node reports by subscribing to a MOOS variable and appending a formatted field to outgoing reports. Common fields include var, rfld, policy, and precision.

right_context.description: Defines a right-click context menu command that posts a MOOS variable-value pair using mouse-position macros such as $(XPOS), $(YPOS), $(LAT), and $(LON).

rn_algorithm.description: Selects the random-noise algorithm applied to simulated contact range measurements. An empty value means no random noise.

rotate_speed.description: Applies an external rotational drift rate, in degrees per second, to simulated heading updates. This is added to heading change independently of rudder-induced turning.

scope.description: Registers one or more MOOS variables for display in the pMarineViewer scope field and Scope menu. Multiple scope lines may be used.
Adds a variable or field description to the uFldScope main report along with source information.

script_atomic.description: If true, a script that has already started continues to completion even if its conditions later become false.

script_name.description: Sets a unique name included in uTimerScript status output for this configured script.

seglist_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_SEGLIST objects are rendered. Segment-list labels remain controlled separately by seglist_viewable_labels.

seglist_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to VIEW_SEGLIST objects are rendered. Segment-list geometry remains controlled separately by seglist_viewable_all.

sensor_arcs.description: Adds angular sensor field-of-view arcs relative to vehicle heading. Multiple arcs may be configured; 360 gives full-circle coverage.

sensor_range.description: Sets the vehicle-to-obstacle range within which simulated obstacle sensor points are generated.

shared_node_reports.description: When true, reposts shared node reports to the shoreside community as NODE_REPORT_UNC.

shuffle.description: Controls whether randomized event timestamps are recalculated on each script reset.

sim_pause.description: Starts or places the simulator in a paused state. While paused, simulated state advancement is suspended until unpaused.

source.description: Restricts displayed variables to mail from the named MOOS source app or apps.

spoof.description: Defines one spoofed node-report request. Position is required; omitted optional fields use the configured default_* values or an auto-generated contact name.

stale_remove_thresh.description: Sets how long after a vehicle becomes stale pMarineViewer waits before removing it from rendering. This affects display cleanup for vehicles that stop reporting.

stale_report_thresh.description: Sets how many seconds without a node report cause a vehicle to be considered stale in pMarineViewer. This affects stale display state, not contact management.

stale_time.description: Sets how long a vehicle may go without a fresh node report before it is considered stale and no longer eligible to receive reports or messages.

start_depth.description: Sets the initial simulated vehicle depth in meters. This is part of the starting vehicle state.

start_heading.description: Sets the initial simulated vehicle heading in degrees. This is part of the starting vehicle state.

start_pos.description: Sets the starting vehicle position using a full position specification. This can initialize multiple state fields in one configuration line.

start_speed.description: Sets the initial simulated vehicle speed in meters per second. This is part of the starting vehicle state.

start_x.description: Sets the initial simulated vehicle x position in local coordinates. This is part of the starting vehicle state.

start_y.description: Sets the initial simulated vehicle y position in local coordinates. This is part of the starting vehicle state.

status_var.description: Names the MOOS variable where uTimerScript posts status summaries.

stealth.description: Reduces how far this vehicle can be heard by other vehicles when sharing node reports. Values are in the range 0 to 1.

strict_addressing.description: When true, handles only messages whose destination node matches the local community; broad group or all-node messages are rejected.

strict_ignore.description: Controls how strictly group/type ignore filters treat contacts that omit those fields. When true, missing group or type information can cause a contact to be rejected by an ignore filter.

summary_interval.description: Sets the time, in seconds, between automatic REALMCAST_CHANNELS summary postings. The local source initializes this to 5 seconds.

summary_wait.description: Sets the maximum time between PROC_WATCH_SUMMARY postings. A negative value posts summaries only when watched process status changes.

temp_file_dir.description: Sets the directory pHostInfo uses for temporary files while discovering host address information. Use a directory path, not an output filename.

terse_reports.description: Omits selected less-essential fields such as YAW from node reports when true. Use this to reduce report size.

thresh.description: Defines an app-specific or catch-all load-gap threshold using fields such as app and gapthresh. When the measured app iteration gap exceeds this multiplier, uLoadWatch can post a load warning.

thrust_factor.description: Sets the simple linear thrust-to-speed conversion factor used when a thrust map is not active. Speed is calculated from thrust divided by this factor.

thrust_map.description: Defines a piecewise mapping between thrust commands and simulated speed. When provided, this map replaces the simple thrust_factor relationship.

thrust_reflect.description: Controls whether negative thrust mirrors positive thrust in the thrust-to-speed mapping. When true, reverse thrust is treated as the opposite of positive thrust.

tiff_file.description: Adds a TIFF background image for the viewer. Current pMarineViewer versions allow multiple tiff_file lines and cycle among them.

tiff_file_b.description: Deprecated second TIFF background image parameter. Prefer multiple tiff_file lines in current pMarineViewer versions.

tiff_type.description: Deprecated parameter no longer supported after Release 22.8.x. Use multiple tiff_file entries and the viewer's image toggle behavior instead.

tiff_viewable.description: Controls whether the configured TIFF background image is rendered; if false, the viewer uses a plain gray background.

time_warp.description: Sets the script-time multiplier used when deciding when configured events are ready to post.

time_zero.description: Selects whether script time zero is app connection/start time or the MOOSDB start time.

trails_color.description: Sets the color used for vehicle trail points behind each rendered vehicle. It affects historical trail rendering, not the live vehicle body color.

trails_connect_viewable.description: Controls whether pMarineViewer connects vehicle trail points with line segments. This can make sparse node-report histories easier to visually follow.

trails_length.description: Sets how many recent position points are retained and rendered for each vehicle trail.

trails_point_size.description: Sets the point size used when rendering vehicle trail points. Legal values are integer sizes in the supported viewer range.

trails_viewable.description: Controls whether vehicle trail histories are rendered behind vehicles. Turning this off hides trail points without hiding the vehicles themselves.

trunc_data.description: Sets the maximum number of characters shown for long string values before uXMS truncates them and appends an ellipsis. A value of 0 disables truncation.

trunc_length.description: Sets the maximum value-field length used when pRealm truncates long realmcast output values.

truncated_output.description: Misspelled legacy spelling for truncated_output; controls whether long MOOS-scope and behavior-scope output is truncated.

try_shore_host.description: Adds a candidate pShare input route used for initial pings while trying to establish a shoreside connection.

try_vnode.description: Adds a candidate vehicle-node route for shore broker connection attempts.

turn_loss.description: Sets the speed-loss factor applied while the simulated vehicle is turning. Values are in the range 0 to 1, where larger values impose more speed loss at high rudder.

turn_rate.description: Sets the nominal vehicle turn-rate authority as a percentage scale from 0 to 100. The simulator clips the value to that range before applying rudder-induced heading changes.

upon_awake.description: Controls what happens when script conditions become true after being false. Supported values include n/a, reset, and restart.

use_unc_reports.description: Uses NODE_REPORT_UNC rather than NODE_REPORT as the vehicle-report source, useful when simulating stale or delayed contact information.

values.description: Lists the possible values for an enumerated random-variable macro used when expanding timer-script event values.

var.description: Adds a MOOS variable to the MOOS-scope section. Multiple var lines may be supplied.
Adds one or more MOOS variables to the uXMS scoping watch list.

vector_viewable_all.description: Controls whether VIEW_VECTOR objects are rendered. Vector labels remain controlled separately by vector_viewable_labels.

vector_viewable_labels.description: Controls whether labels attached to VIEW_VECTOR objects are rendered. Vector geometry remains controlled separately by vector_viewable_all.

vehicles_active_color.description: Sets the color used for the active vehicle whose data is shown in the viewer data fields.

vehicles_inactive_color.description: Sets the display color used for vehicles that are not the currently selected active vehicle. The active vehicle color is controlled separately by vehicles_active_color.

vehicles_name_color.description: Sets the color used for vehicle name labels. The label content itself is controlled by vehicles_name_mode.

vehicles_name_mode.description: Sets what information appears in vehicle labels, such as only names, names with mode/depth, or no labels.

vehicles_shape_scale.description: Applies a scale factor to rendered vehicle shapes. This changes visual vehicle size without changing reported vehicle state.

vehicles_viewable.description: Controls whether vehicle icons are rendered from incoming node reports. Turning this off hides vehicles while other geometry such as polygons and points may remain visible.

verbose.description: Enables verbose terminal status output from uFldNodeComms.
Controls whether uTimerScript writes progress information to the console.

view_center.description: Sets the initial center of the viewer image in local x,y coordinates. This controls the startup pan origin.

view_node_rpt_pulses.description: Controls whether uFldNodeComms posts visual communication pulses for successfully shared node reports.

wait.description: Waits this many seconds for queried variables to appear before uQueryDB exits with failure when an initial query is missing.

warning_on_stale.description: When true, posts a warning if a previously known node has not been heard from for more than the stale-node interval.

watch.description: Names a process that uProcessWatch should monitor and report as running or missing. Optional mappings may associate a watched process with a status variable.

watch_all.description: Controls whether uProcessWatch automatically monitors processes discovered from DB_CLIENTS and the Antler launch list.

wrap_data.description: Controls whether long variable values are wrapped across multiple lines in uXMS output.

wrap_data_len.description: Sets the line length used when uXMS wraps long variable values.

wrap_length.description: Sets the number of characters per line when pRealm wraps long variable values in realmcast output.
